title: ForgeBrain Reel Script
description: The structured, spoken-word script produced by the Script Generator from a lesson blueprint and a Content Director strategy. Produced after the Content Director (see content-director-spec.md) and consumed by the Reviewer and Storyboard Generator. See script-spec.md for the full process this schema supports.

topic_id.description: Matches the corresponding id in curriculum/java-roadmap.json and the topic_id of the lesson and content strategy this script was built from.

topic_title.description: Matches the corresponding title in curriculum/java-roadmap.json.

variant_id.description: Identifies this script among possible variants for the same topic_id. 'primary' unless this is an A/B test variant. See script-spec.md Section 9 for how future variants use this.

hook.description: The actual spoken opening line(s). Must structurally match content_director_output.hook_type — see script-spec.md Section 5.

intro_line.description: One short bridging line from the hook into the teaching body.

main_script.description: The ordered teaching-body beats, excluding the code-display moment (see code_narration) and the recap/CTA. Each beat is one short spoken line.

recap_line.description: The one-sentence spoken takeaway just before the CTA. Should read as a spoken version of the lesson's beginner_takeaway.

cta_line.description: The actual spoken call-to-action line, matching content_director_output.cta_style.

full_spoken_script.description: The complete narration, in delivery order, as a single string ready for voiceover or TTS ingestion. Derived by concatenating hook, intro_line, main_script lines, code_narration.spoken_lines, recap_line, and cta_line in the order they're actually delivered (see script-spec.md Section 3 for when code_narration is reordered). Must not introduce content absent from those fields.

scene_text.description: Short on-screen text overlays (keywords/captions, not full sentences), aligned to the Content Director's scene_pacing entries.

subtitle_segments.description: Phrase-level segmentation of full_spoken_script, in delivery order, ready for subtitle rendering.

estimated_duration_seconds.description: word_count divided by an assumed speaking rate (~2.5 words/second baseline; see script-spec.md Section 6), cross-checked against target_duration_seconds.

tone.description: The delivery tone, informed by content_director_output.emotional_goal and brand_voice.

hook_type.description: Echoed from content_director_output.hook_type. Must match exactly — the Script Generator does not choose a different hook type than the one it was given.

teaching_style.description: Echoed from content_director_output.teaching_style. Must match exactly.

audience_level.description: Carried from the lesson/content strategy unless explicitly overridden.

platform.description: Target platform, informing minor phrasing/format conventions (e.g. CTA wording). generic-vertical-short is the default when no specific platform is targeted.

script_version.description: Version of the script process/schema that produced this script, for future caching and migration.

based_on_lesson_version.description: The lesson_version of the lesson blueprint this script was built from, for traceability.

based_on_content_director_version.description: The content_director_version of the content strategy this script was built from, for traceability.

beat.description: Short label for this beat's role (e.g. 'concept setup', 'key contrast'), for alignment with the Content Director's scene_pacing.

spoken_line.description: The actual short spoken sentence for this beat.

codeNarration.description: Narration and on-screen code for the moment code is displayed. May be delivered anywhere in the sequence — see script-spec.md Section 3 for how teaching_style affects its position.

spoken_lines.description: 1-3 short spoken lines narrating the code as it's shown.

code_snippet.description: The actual on-screen code, carried and trimmed from the lesson's core_example.code_sketch for a vertical-video-sized display.

focus_line.description: Which line or part of code_snippet to highlight while this narration plays, carried from the lesson's core_example.focus_note or the content strategy's visual cue.

scene_reference.description: Should correspond to a scene label from the Content Director's scene_pacing.

text.description: Short on-screen phrase or keyword, not a full sentence.

order.description: Sequential position in the delivery order.

text.description: One short sentence or clause of spoken text, sized for a single subtitle card.

source_field.description: Which script field this subtitle text was drawn from, for traceability.

estimated_duration_seconds.description: Approximate on-screen duration for this subtitle card, derived from its word count at the same speaking rate as estimated_duration_seconds.

emphasis_words.description: 0-2 words in this segment's text that should be visually emphasized (e.g. bolded or highlighted) in the rendered subtitle.

overall_confidence.description: Coarse self-assessment of how well this script fits its duration budget and strategy fidelity.

flagged_uncertainties.description: Phrasing or fidelity judgment calls (e.g. a line that runs slightly long, or a strategy element that was hard to satisfy exactly), flagged for reviewer attention.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Reserved for future multi-variant or brand-voice conflicts. Empty until relevant.
